message:
Add daemon health check endpoint (bd-146)

- Add OpHealth RPC operation to protocol
- Implement handleHealth() with DB ping and 1s timeout
- Returns status (healthy/degraded/unhealthy), uptime, cache metrics
- Update TryConnect() to use health check instead of ping
- Add 'bd daemon --health' CLI command with JSON output
- Track cache hits/misses for metrics
- Unhealthy daemon triggers automatic fallback to direct mode
- Health check completes in <2 seconds

Amp-Thread-ID: https://ampcode.com/threads/T-1a4889f3-77cf-433a-a704-e1c383929f48
Co-authored-by: Amp <amp@ampcode.com>

files.patch:
@@ -50,7 +50,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-143","title":"Bias ready work towards recent issues before oldest-first","description":"Currently 'bd ready' shows oldest issues first (by created_at). This can bury recently discovered work that might be more relevant. Propose a hybrid approach: show issues from the past 1-2 days first (sorted by priority), then fall back to oldest-first for older issues. This keeps fresh discoveries visible while still surfacing old forgotten work.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T09:31:15.036495-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T09:57:28.105887-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T09:35:55.084891-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-144","title":"Fix nil pointer dereference in renumber command","description":"The 'bd renumber' command crashes with a nil pointer dereference at renumber.go:52 because store is nil. The command doesn't properly handle daemon/direct mode initialization like other commands do. Error occurs on both --dry-run and --force modes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-18T09:54:31.59912-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T09:57:28.106373-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T09:56:49.88701-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-145","title":"Add storage cache eviction policy to daemon","description":"Daemon caches DB connections forever in storageCache map (server.go:29). For users with 50+ repos, this causes memory leaks and file descriptor exhaustion.

Need LRU cache with:
- Max size limit (default: 50 repos)
- TTL-based eviction (default: 30min idle)
- Periodic cleanup goroutine

Location: internal/rpc/server.go:29-40","design":"Add StorageCacheEntry struct with lastAccess timestamp.

Implement evictStaleStorage() method that runs every 5 minutes to close connections idle >30min.

Add max cache size enforcement (LRU eviction when full).

Make limits configurable via env vars:
- BEADS_DAEMON_MAX_CACHE_SIZE (default: 50)
- BEADS_DAEMON_CACHE_TTL (default: 30m)","acceptance_criteria":"- Cache evicts entries after 30min idle
- Cache respects max size limit
- Cleanup goroutine runs periodically
- Evicted storage connections are properly closed
- No resource leaks under sustained load
- Unit tests for eviction logic","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:05:46.174245-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:16:56.921023-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T13:16:56.921023-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-145","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.077954-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-146","title":"Add daemon health check endpoint and probes","description":"Auto-start only checks socket existence, not daemon responsiveness. Daemon can be running but unresponsive (deadlock, hung DB). Users work in degraded direct mode without knowing why.

Need health check RPC operation that:
- Tests DB connectivity (1s timeout)
- Returns uptime, status, metrics
- Used by auto-start before connecting
- Enables monitoring/alerting

Location: internal/rpc/server.go, cmd/bd/main.go:100-108","design":"Add OpHealth RPC operation to protocol.

handleHealth() implementation:
- Quick DB ping with 1s timeout
- Return status, uptime, version
- Include basic metrics (connections, cache size)

Update TryConnect() to call Health() after socket connection:
- If health check fails, close connection and return nil
- Enables transparent failover to direct mode

Add 'bd daemon --health' CLI command for monitoring.","acceptance_criteria":"- Health check RPC endpoint works
- Returns structured health status
- Client uses health check before operations
- bd daemon --health command exists
- Unhealthy daemon triggers auto-restart or fallback
- Health check completes in <2 seconds","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:05:58.647592-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:05:58.647592-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-146","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.093618-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

Need health check RPC operation that:
- Tests DB connectivity (1s timeout)
- Returns uptime, status, metrics
- Used by auto-start before connecting
- Enables monitoring/alerting

Location: internal/rpc/server.go, cmd/bd/main.go:100-108","design":"Add OpHealth RPC operation to protocol.

handleHealth() implementation:
- Quick DB ping with 1s timeout
- Return status, uptime, version
- Include basic metrics (connections, cache size)

Update TryConnect() to call Health() after socket connection:
- If health check fails, close connection and return nil
- Enables transparent failover to direct mode

Add 'bd daemon --health' CLI command for monitoring.","acceptance_criteria":"- Health check RPC endpoint works
- Returns structured health status
- Client uses health check before operations
- bd daemon --health command exists
- Unhealthy daemon triggers auto-restart or fallback
- Health check completes in <2 seconds","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:05:58.647592-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:32:15.106003-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-18T13:32:15.106003-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-146","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.093618-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-147","title":"Add stale socket and crash recovery for daemon","description":"When daemon crashes (panic, OOM, signal), socket file remains and blocks new daemon start. Users must manually remove .beads/bd.sock.

Problems:
- Socket file remains after crash
- PID file remains (isDaemonRunning false positive)
- No automatic recovery
- Users get 'daemon already running' error

Location: cmd/bd/daemon.go, cmd/bd/main.go:221-311","design":"Improve stale detection in tryAutoStartDaemon():

1. If socket exists, try to connect
2. If connection fails → stale socket, remove it
3. Also remove PID file and lock files
4. Retry daemon start

Add self-healing to daemon startup:
- On startup, check for stale PID files
- If PID in file doesn't exist, remove and continue
- Use exclusive file lock to prevent races

Optional: Add crash recovery watchdog that restarts daemon on exit.","acceptance_criteria":"- Stale sockets are automatically detected and removed
- Auto-start recovers from daemon crashes
- No manual intervention needed for crash recovery
- PID file management is robust
- Lock files prevent multiple daemon instances
- Tests for crash recovery scenarios","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:10.116917-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:10.116917-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-147","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.108099-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-148","title":"Add lifecycle management for beads-mcp processes","description":"MCP server processes accumulate without cleanup. Each tool invocation spawns a new Python process that lingers after Claude disconnects.

Observed: 6+ beads-mcp processes running simultaneously.

Problems:
- No parent-child relationship tracking
- No cleanup on MCP client disconnect
- Processes leak over days of use
- Could accumulate hundreds of processes

Location: integrations/beads-mcp/src/beads_mcp/server.py","design":"Add proper cleanup handlers to MCP server:

1. Register atexit handler to close daemon connections
2. Handle SIGTERM/SIGINT for graceful shutdown
3. Close daemon client in cleanup()
4. Remove any temp files

Optional improvements:
- Track active connections to daemon
- Implement connection pooling
- Add process timeout/TTL
- Log lifecycle events for debugging

Example:
import atexit
import signal

def cleanup():
    # Close daemon connections
    # Remove temp files
    pass

atexit.register(cleanup)
signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, lambda s, f: cleanup())","acceptance_criteria":"- MCP processes clean up on exit
- Daemon connections are properly closed
- No process leaks after repeated use
- Signal handlers work correctly
- Cleanup runs on normal and abnormal exit
- Test with multiple concurrent MCP invocations","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:22.030027-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:22.030027-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-148","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.121494-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-149","title":"Add global daemon auto-start support","description":"Auto-start only works for local daemon. Users with multiple repos must manually run 'bd daemon --global'.

Problems:
- No detection of whether global daemon is preferable
- No migration path from local → global
- Multi-repo users don't discover global daemon
- Manual setup required

Location: cmd/bd/main.go:221-311","design":"Add heuristics to shouldUseGlobalDaemon():

1. Count .beads repos under home directory
2. If >3 repos found, prefer global daemon
3. Check BEADS_PREFER_GLOBAL_DAEMON env var
4. Check config file preference

Update tryAutoStartDaemon() to:
- Use shouldUseGlobalDaemon() to pick mode
- Pass --global flag when appropriate
- Log decision for debugging

Add migration helper:
- Detect running local daemon
- Suggest switching to global if multi-repo detected
- bd daemon --migrate-to-global command","acceptance_criteria":"- Auto-start uses global daemon when appropriate
- Multi-repo detection works correctly
- Users can configure preference
- Migration path is smooth
- Both local and global auto-start work
- Documentation updated","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:33.633238-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-18T13:06:33.633238-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-149","depends_on_id":"bd-155","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-18T13:07:49.135552-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
